This reads as a single ten-track instrumental beat tape — lofi, hip-hop,
and EDM textures, every cut under three minutes, consistent with a
beat-tape convention rather than a pop single run.

---

## 1. Believe in Everything — 1:48

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

An unguarded, all-in title to open a tape with — the kind of opening
statement that sets an optimistic key for whatever follows, before the
tracklist has a chance to complicate it.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, opener

---

## 2. In Art We Trust — 1:35

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

A maker's-creed title — reads like a producer's mission statement
compressed into a beat, likely leaning on a sample or vocal chop that
carries the phrase itself.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, sample-forward

---

## 3. When They Come — 1:54

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

The most tense phrasing on the tape — "when," not "if." Positioned
mid-tracklist, this reads as the tape's minor-key turn, a beat built on
anticipation rather than release.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, tense, minor-key-signal

---

## 4. Come to Age — 1:45

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

A coming-of-age title dropped into an instrumental tape without lyrics to
spell it out — the mood has to carry the theme alone, likely through a
more contemplative, slower-breathing loop than its neighbors.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, contemplative

---

## 5. Silent Night — 2:39

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

The longest cut on the tape and the most direct title-reference in the
catalog — almost certainly a flip of the carol's melody rather than a
coincidence of naming, given the runtime headroom to let a familiar theme
breathe.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, melodic-reference, longest-track

---

## 6. Keep it Moving — 1:38

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

A tempo instruction hiding as a title — the tape's midpoint pivot back to
momentum after "Silent Night's" held breath, likely the most uptempo cut
relative to its neighbors.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, uptempo-signal

---

## 7. Make Me Melt — 1:32

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

The shortest track on the tape and the warmest title — "melt" suggests a
softer, more romantic register than the rest of the tracklist, compressed
into the tape's briefest runtime.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, warm, shortest-track

---

## 8. Just Do It — 1:58

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

A declarative, motivational title — likely another momentum cut, and at
just under two minutes it has the room to build rather than just state its
case in a single loop.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, motivational

---

## 9. And It Was — 2:45

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

The second-longest track and the tape's most retrospective phrasing — an
"and it was" reads like a closing thought rather than an opening one,
positioned second-to-last for exactly that reason.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, retrospective

---

## 10. There We Go — 1:47

*Confidence: metadata-informed*

A casual, satisfied closer's title — "there we go" is the sound of a tape
finishing exactly where it meant to, the tracklist's last word rather than
its climax.

**Tags:** instrumental, lofi, beat-tape, closer
